---
title: "Gromov–Hausdorff Distances"
subtitle: "Quantitative comparison of metric spaces and metric graphs."
image: ../images/plate-gh.svg
nocite: |
    @majhi2023GH, @HvsGH
---

The Gromov&ndash;Hausdorff distance $d_{GH}(X, Y)$ measures how far two abstract metric spaces are from being isometric. It is theoretically central to topological data analysis&mdash;and computationally hard. The work in this project produces *polynomial-time computable lower bounds* and approximation algorithms, and quantifies how the GH distance compares to its more tractable cousin, the Hausdorff distance.

Recent contributions:

- ***[Lower Bounding the Gromov–Hausdorff Distance in Metric Graphs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.09182)***&mdash;with Henry Adams, Fedya Manin, Žiga Virk, Nicolò Zava; tractable lower bounds for metric graphs.
- ***Hausdorff vs Gromov–Hausdorff Distances***&mdash;with Henry Adams, Florian Frick, Nicholas McBride; sharp inequalities relating the two distances on Riemannian manifolds.
- ***Approximating the Gromov&ndash;Hausdorff distance in Euclidean space***&mdash;an efficient approximation scheme.

## Collaborators
- Henry Adams, University of Florida
- Žiga Virk, University of Ljubljana
- Nicolò Zava, IST Austria
- Fedya Manin, University of Toronto
- Florian Frick, Carnegie Mellon University *(past)*
- Nicholas McBride, UC Santa Cruz *(past)*

## Publications
::: {#refs}
:::
